# imported from CLAUDE.md (auto-regenerated by /teams:init — edit CLAUDE.md, not this block)
- [must-fix] Forbidden zones: `dist/`, `public/`, `index.html`, `vite.config.ts`, `tsconfig*.json`, `.github/workflows/` — do not edit. (`dist/` per AGENTS.md §Project Structure; the rest gated by user via `/teams:init`.)
- [must-fix] Dependency policy: none declared in CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md. Treat as conservative — no new runtime deps without an explicit plan step. The codebase deliberately avoids runtime validation libraries (Zod, etc.) and DnD libraries (react-dnd, dnd-kit) — see `src/infrastructure/persistence/` and `src/features/board/components/TaskCard.tsx` for the established hand-rolled patterns.
- [must-fix] Naming conventions: PascalCase for React components and component files (e.g. `TaskCard.tsx`); `use*` for hooks; `camelCase.ts` for plain modules (e.g. `boardSlice.ts`, `priorityMeta.ts`); PascalCase for types with no `I-` prefix; business logic lives in `src/domain/`, browser-storage details under `src/infrastructure/persistence/`. (AGENTS.md §Coding Style & Naming Conventions, §Project Structure & Module Organization.)
- [must-fix] Error-handling pattern: none declared as text in CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md, but the codebase has a clear convention — boundary serializers (`parseBoard`, `parseWorkspace` in `src/infrastructure/persistence/`) **gracefully degrade** on partial corruption (drop invalid tasks, default to `'medium'` priority, fall back to empty workspace) using hand-written type guards (`isRecord`, `isColumnId`, …); they do **not** throw. Pure use-cases in `src/application/boardUseCases.ts` return new `Board` values; they do not throw on missing entities (e.g. unknown task ids are silently no-op). Match this style.
# end imported

## Team-wide
- [must-fix] No new dependencies without an explicit plan step or user approval.
- [must-fix] No silencing of lint or type-check rules to make exit gates pass.
- [must-fix] All design choices must cite the brief, the plan, or `CLAUDE.md` when challenged.
- [should-fix] Prefer Serena symbol tools (`find_symbol`, `replace_symbol_body`, …) over `Read`/`Grep`/`Edit` when changing whole symbols.

## Project context
- Language: TypeScript 5.9
- Test roots: n/a (no tests configured; AGENTS.md prescribes `*.test.ts` / `*.test.tsx` colocated, or `src/**/__tests__/` when added)
- Public-API entry points: n/a (single-page app — runtime entry is `src/main.tsx`; the persisted-shape "API" is `src/infrastructure/persistence/workspaceSerializer.ts` + `boardSerializer.ts`, with `Workspace.schemaVersion` / `Board.schemaVersion` as the contract)

## Senior-specialist baseline (applies to every agent — calibrate to a strong senior engineer, not a junior contributor)
These rules are the team's canonical bar. Per-role files reinforce them with role-specific tactics; they do **not** restate them.

- [must-fix] Root cause > symptom. Never paper over a bug with try/catch, fallback, default, or special-case. If you can't explain *why* the bug existed, the fix is incomplete — keep digging or push back.
- [must-fix] YAGNI. No abstractions, generics, interfaces, strategy patterns, dependency-injection seams, or config knobs without a second concrete caller **in the same change**. Three duplicated lines beat a premature abstraction.
- [must-fix] Smallest possible change. The diff matches the requested task — no "while I was here" refactors, formatting churn in untouched files, or unrelated dep bumps. Surface tangential cleanups in `phase-N-result.md` under "Out-of-scope follow-ups", not in the diff.
- [must-fix] No half-finished implementations. Every reachable code path is fully implemented, or explicitly rejected with a typed error / `UnimplementedError` / `panic!` / `throw` plus a one-line note. No silent no-ops, no empty `catch` blocks, no `return null` "for now". (Exception: established graceful-degradation in serializers — see imported error-handling rule.)
- [must-fix] No backwards-compatibility shims for code with no callers. When you remove a symbol, also remove its forwarders, deprecated aliases, type re-exports, and "removed in vX" comments — in the same change.
- [must-fix] Trust the type system. Do not add runtime checks for invariants the compiler/analyzer already enforces (null guards on non-nullable params, type checks on already-narrowed values, length guards on fixed-size arrays). Validate **only** at system boundaries (user input, network, disk, FFI, deserialization). For this project the boundaries are: DOM event handlers, `localStorage` reads (already covered by the persistence serializers), and `crypto.randomUUID()` ID generation.
- [must-fix] Comments default to **none**. Justify a comment only when the *why* is non-obvious — a hidden constraint, a workaround for a specific bug, behavior that would surprise a future reader. Never explain *what* the code does (well-named identifiers do that). Never reference the current task / PR / brief / phase ("added for X", "used by Y", "TODO from issue #123") — these rot the moment the change ships.
- [must-fix] No leftover scaffolding: no `print` / `console.log` / `debugPrint` / `println!` / `fmt.Println`, no commented-out code, no unused imports, no `TODO` / `FIXME` / `XXX` without an explicit plan citation. (Project-specific: `noUnusedLocals` and `noUnusedParameters` are on in `tsconfig.app.json`, so unused imports already fail the type-check gate — but the reviewer should still flag them when caught earlier.)
- [should-fix] Match existing patterns. Consistency with the rest of the codebase outranks personal preference; novelty needs a written justification.
- [should-fix] Read before write. Before editing a symbol, read its current definition and at least one call site (`find_referencing_symbols`). Do not edit by inference from grep results alone.
